files.purpose: DuckDB service + client — read-only CLI subprocess with connection pool

keyFunctions.name: execSQL
keyFunctions.description: Spawns duckdb CLI with -readonly flag (non-:memory:), executes single SQL, returns JSON parsed

keyFunctions.name: DuckDBConnectionPool.run
keyFunctions.description: Queue-based pool, max 4 concurrent subprocesses

keyFunctions.name: createCLIClient
keyFunctions.description: Factory — wraps pool in DuckDBRawClient { all, get, run, close }

keyFunctions.name: init
keyFunctions.description: Creates client — tries WASM first (returns null), falls back to CLI

keyFunctions.name: layer
keyFunctions.description: Layer.effect — creates client, reports health, runs pipeline in bg if dbPath is set

readonlyFlag.description: execSQL uses -readonly for non-:memory: paths, rejects writes at subprocess level
readonlyFlag.issue: DDL sent through this path (e.g., from projection worker) silently fails

effectIgnoreOnPipeline.issue: All pipeline errors silenced at top level

memorySkip.description: When dbPath is undefined (':memory:'), pipeline is skipped entirely
memorySkip.issue: No pipeline data means no context projections in :memory: mode

files.purpose: Analytical schema definitions — DDL strings + buildContextProjections

keyFunctions.name: buildContextProjections
keyFunctions.description: Reads runtime_events from transactional DB, pipes into DuckDB via execDuckDBStdin, builds aggregated tables

keyFunctions.name: execDuckDB
keyFunctions.description: Spawns duckdb WITHOUT -readonly — for pipeline DDL/write ops

keyFunctions.name: execDuckDBStdin
keyFunctions.description: Spawns duckdb WITHOUT -readonly with stdin JSON data

keyFunctions.name: initTablesSql
keyFunctions.description: Returns ALL_TABLES joined by semicolons

keyFunctions.name: initViewsSql
keyFunctions.description: Returns ALL_VIEWS + RUNTIME_EVENT_VIEWS

noReadonlyOnDDL.description: execDuckDB and execDuckDBStdin spawn duckdb WITHOUT -readonly
noReadonlyOnDDL.note: This is intentional — these are pipeline/internal write functions. But buildContextProjections calls them.

memoryEarlyReturn.issue: Silently skips all context projections in :memory: mode

errorsSwallowed.description: buildContextProjections wraps errors in Effect.catch with log-only
errorsSwallowed.issue: Failed context projections become invisible — no metric, no alert

files.purpose: DuckDB pipeline — exports Postgres data, creates tables/views in DuckDB

keyFunctions.name: runPipeline
keyFunctions.description: 7-step pipeline: meta guard → export sessions → export parts → export events → create tables → create views → build projections → write meta

keyFunctions.name: execDuckDB
keyFunctions.description: Spawns duckdb WITHOUT -readonly for pipeline write ops

keyFunctions.name: execDuckDBStdin
keyFunctions.description: Same with stdin JSON data

keyFunctions.name: metaCheck
keyFunctions.description: Checks _pipeline_meta for existing schema_version

keyFunctions.name: layer
keyFunctions.description: Creates Service, runs pipeline in background fiber with Effect.ignore

memoryEarlyReturn.issue: No pipeline data in :memory: mode — all analytical queries hit empty DB

pipelineErrorSilenced.description: Top-level pipeline error is caught and logged as non-fatal only
pipelineErrorSilenced.issue: Pipeline can fail silently during app startup

layerIgnore.description: Layer effect forkScoped + Effect.ignore double-silences pipeline errors

files.purpose: Worker 4 — subscribes to event_projections invalidation, rebuilds DuckDB views

keyViews.name: _ctx_file_relevance
keyViews.description: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW — file relevance from _pipeline_runtime_event

keyViews.name: _ctx_file_cochange
keyViews.description: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW — co-occurrence analysis

keyViews.name: _ctx_session_activity
keyViews.description: CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW — session activity summary

keyFunctions.name: layer
keyFunctions.description: Layer.effectDiscard — subscribes to bus, throttled at 5s, runs DDL via DuckDB.Service

criticalIssues.description: Projection worker sends DDL (CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW) through DuckDB.Service.run() which uses -readonly subprocess flag
criticalIssues.rootCause: DuckDB.Service in db.duckdb.ts uses -readonly flag (line 60). CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW is a write operation and is rejected by the duckdb CLI with -readonly.
criticalIssues.effect: Views are never actually created when using non-:memory: paths. All context projection queries return empty/no-such-table errors — which are also silenced by Effect.ignore.
criticalIssues.fixRequired: Split run() from the read-only client: either add a write-capable method or route projection DDL through the pipeline's execDuckDB (no -readonly).

criticalIssues.description: Name collision on _ctx_file_relevance between schema.duckdb.ts (TABLE) and duckdb-projection-worker.ts (VIEW)
criticalIssues.rootCause: schema.duckdb.ts defines CTX_FILE_RELEVANCE_TABLE as CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _ctx_file_relevance. duckdb-projection-worker.ts defines the same name as CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW _ctx_file_relevance. In DuckDB, tables and views share the same namespace.
criticalIssues.effect: Whichever runs second overwrites the first. If pipeline runs first (table created), then projection worker would fail with 'a table already exists with that name' — but that error is silenced by Effect.ignore. If projection worker runs first (view created), pipeline's CREATE TABLE would fail silently.
criticalIssues.fixRequired: Rename one of them. Use _ctx_file_relevance_mv for the materialized table version or _ctx_file_relevance_v for the view version.

criticalIssues.description: :memory: mode silently skips all DuckDB pipeline and projection work, errors swallowed by Effect.ignore chains
criticalIssues.rootCause: 4 layers of error silencing: (1) :memory: early return in runPipeline, (2) :memory: early return in buildContextProjections, (3) Effect.ignore on forkScoped in db.duckdb.ts layer, (4) Effect.ignore on each projection DDL run in worker
criticalIssues.effect: In :memory: mode, DuckDB has no tables or views — all analytical queries fail. The failures are completely invisible.
criticalIssues.fixRequired: Remove Effect.ignore chain: log errors properly, add telemetry metrics for DuckDB health, make projection worker aware of whether views were actually created.

criticalIssues.description: _ctx_agent_heatmap referenced in duckdb-rank.ts:191 queryAgentHeatmap() but never defined in any schema DDL or projection worker
criticalIssues.rootCause: queryAgentHeatmap() at duckdb-rank.ts:191 queries 'FROM _ctx_agent_heatmap' but no CREATE TABLE or CREATE VIEW statement defines this table anywhere in the codebase.
criticalIssues.effect: Runtime query failure - 'table not found' error, wrapped in Effect.tryPromise and caught as DatabaseError. Agents cannot use the agent heatmap context feature.
criticalIssues.fixRequired: Either add _ctx_agent_heatmap DDL to schema.duckdb.ts or remove the dead code from duckdb-rank.ts.

files.purpose: Database adapter — wraps DuckDB in DrizzleLikeClient for read-only query interface

keyFunctions.name: makeDuckDBAdapter
keyFunctions.description: Creates DrizzleLikeClient wrapper — all(), get(), run() pass through firewall; insert/update/delete/transaction throw DatabaseError

keyTypes.name: DrizzleLikeClient
keyTypes.description: Interface with select, insert, update, delete, transaction, run, all, get

DuckDBAdapterLayer.description: DuckDBAdapter layer dynamically imports db.duckdb init — the only write path
DuckDBAdapterLayer.note: Used to create DuckDB clients for the analytical read path

files.purpose: DuckDB SQL firewall — blocks dangerous functions in read-only queries

keyFunctions.name: checkSQLFirewall
keyFunctions.description: Strips comments/strings, checks blocked functions (read_text, ATTACH, LOAD, EXPORT) and COPY…TO pattern

keyExports.name: BLOCKED_FUNCTIONS
keyExports.description: read_text, read_blob, read_csv, read_parquet, read_json, query_table, ATTACH, load_extension, LOAD, EXPORT

files.note: This is defense-in-depth. The subprocess -readonly flag is the primary barrier. The firewall is an application-layer guard applied in db.duckdb.ts execSQL and adapter.ts makeDuckDBAdapter.

duckdbReadWriteSplit.summary: 3 DuckDB issues confirmed: (1) Read-only subprocess used for DDL — projection worker sends CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW through -readonly flagged duckdb CLI, silently failing. (2) Name collision on _ctx_file_relevance — schema.duckdb defines it as TABLE, duckdb-projection-worker defines it as VIEW, both in same namespace. (3) :memory: isolation with 4 layers of Effect.ignore — when using :memory: mode, all pipeline, projections, and context views silently disappear with no visibility into failures.

files.purpose: Context packet assembly — L1 (mission snapshot) and L2 (detailed context)

keyTypes.name: ContextPacketL1
keyTypes.description: PACKET_VERSION=1. Fields: mission, authority, workspace (branch, dirtyFiles, externalChangesDetected), claims, latestValidation, lastError, _freshness

keyTypes.name: ContextPacketL2
keyTypes.description: L2 fields: activities, files, events, tokens, errors

keyTypes.name: ContextPacket
keyTypes.description: l1: ContextPacketL1, l2?: ContextPacketL2, packetVersion: number

keyFunctions.name: assembleL1
keyFunctions.description: Assembles L1 packet — queries Git for branch/dirtyFiles, EventStore for claims/validation, Authority for permissions

keyFunctions.name: assembleL2
keyFunctions.description: Assembles L2 with activity, file patterns, events timeline

keyFunctions.name: getCurrentPacket
keyFunctions.description: Calls assembleL1 + optionally assembleL2, returns ContextPacket

freshnessType.definition: export interface Freshness { fetchedAt: string; eventLagMs: number }
freshnessType.note: Only two fields — timestamp of packet creation and milliseconds since most recent event

eventLagMsSource.code: const eventLagMs = events.length > 0 ? Date.now() - new Date(events[0].ts).getTime() : 0
eventLagMsSource.note: eventLagMs is time since the most recent event's timestamp. If no events, lag is 0. This only measures time since the last recorded event — not file content freshness.

externalChangesDetected.issue: dirtyFiles includes ALL files modified in the working tree — including files the agent itself just edited via tools. There's no separation between 'files the agent changed' (intentional) and 'files changed externally' (by user, another agent, IDE). The field conflates both into a single boolean.

conflictField.definition: claims.conflicts: string[] — subset of dirtyFiles that are also in ownedFiles
conflictField.note: This IS the differentiated signal — a file is 'in conflict' if the agent claims ownership AND it's in the dirty set. But externalChangesDetected is the coarse boolean that agents likely use for the 'should I rebuild context?' decision.

freshnessIssues.description: Freshness is purely temporal (fetchedAt timestamp + eventLagMs since last event). There is no content-based freshness — no file content digest comparison, no git tree hash, no checksum of anything that tracks whether the underlying data has changed since the packet was created.
freshnessIssues.impact: An agent cannot determine if a packet is stale in the sense of 'the files I was told about have been modified since'. They can only see 'this packet was fetched N ms ago'.

freshnessIssues.description: externalChangesDetected = (dirtyFiles.length > 0) conflates agent edits with external changes. If the agent writes to 3 files, all 3 show up as dirty, and externalChangesDetected is true — even though nothing external happened.
freshnessIssues.fixRequired: Subtract files the agent has edited (from FileMemory.lastEditor or from claims.owned) from the dirty set before computing externalChangesDetected. Only non-agent dirt = truly external.

freshnessIssues.description: ContextPacket has no content hashes of the workspace state at packet creation time. Agents could compare FileMemory digests against current disk hashes to determine if specific files changed, but there's no checksum of the overall context data fed into the packet.

packetFreshness.summary: Packet freshness is temporal-only (fetchedAt + eventLagMs). externalChangesDetected conflates agent edits with external changes (dirtyFiles.length > 0). The conflict field (claims.conflicts) has the differentiated signal but externalChangesDetected doesn't use it. No content-based freshness mechanism.

files.purpose: In-memory file context store — tracks file metadata, digests, edits, and stale detection

keyTypes.name: FileContext
keyTypes.description: Fields: path, digest (sha256), lastReadAt, lastEditedAt, lastEditor, lastEditTool, lineCount, language, symbols[], imports[], exports[], knownTests[], riskTags[]

keyTypes.name: Interface
keyTypes.description: Methods: get, set, invalidate, refresh, getStale, search, remove, getAll, getByEditor

keyFunctions.name: inferLanguage
keyFunctions.description: Maps file extension to language string

keyFunctions.name: extractSymbols
keyFunctions.description: Regex-based extraction of top-level exports/declarations

keyFunctions.name: extractImports
keyFunctions.description: Regex-based import path extraction

keyFunctions.name: extractExports
keyFunctions.description: Regex-based export extraction

keyFunctions.name: isGeneratedFile
keyFunctions.description: Detects generated files by path pattern

keyFunctions.name: isProtectedFile
keyFunctions.description: Detects protected/configuration files

keyFunctions.name: computeRiskTags
keyFunctions.description: Risk tags based on path patterns (config, db, migration, etc.)

keyFunctions.name: refresh
keyFunctions.description: Creates/updates FileContext with new content — computes digest, symbols, imports, exports, risk tags

stateManagement.description: In-memory mutable map keyed by file path

staleDetection.note: getStale is temporal-only — checks if a file hasn't been read/refreshed within the threshold. Does NOT compare digests against the filesystem.

relationshipWithContext.description: FileMemory stores per-file context (digest, symbols, edits). The ContextPacket L1/L2 are assembled from separate sources (Git, EventStore, Authority). FileMemory 's content could be used to cross-check file freshness but isn't consulted by the packet assembly code.
relationshipWithContext.gap: ContextPacket.assembleL1 doesn't use FileMemory at all. The packet freshness is purely event-based and git-dirty-based, not based on whether file content has actually changed.

fileMemory.summary: FileMemory is an in-memory file metadata store with sha256 digests, symbols/imports/exports extraction, and temporal stale detection. It is NOT consulted by the ContextPacket assembly — the packet has its own independent freshness mechanism (temporal only). FileMemory's digests could be used to detect actual content changes, but this is not wired up.

crossCuttingFindings.description: ContextPacket freshness doesn't reflect DuckDB view state. There's no mechanism to know if the DuckDB projection views (which power context queries) are up-to-date or stale relative to the transactional database.
crossCuttingFindings.impact: An agent could receive a 'fresh' packet with stale analytical data if DuckDB views haven't been rebuilt after new events.

crossCuttingFindings.description: FileMemory is fully standalone — it's never referenced by packet.ts, the invalidation bus, or the DuckDB pipeline. Its sha256 digests would be useful for content-based freshness but are unused outside its own module.
crossCuttingFindings.impact: Wasted opportunity: FileMemory digests could feed into context freshness checks, but the architecture doesn't connect them.

crossCuttingFindings.description: execDuckDB/execDuckDBStdin are duplicated verbatim in both pipeline.ts (lines 116-195) and schema.duckdb.ts (lines 478-537). Same code, different minor defaults (60s timeout vs signal handling).
crossCuttingFindings.impact: Code duplication — bug fixes or timeout adjustments must be applied to both copies.

duckdbReadWriteSplit.description: Fix projection worker DDL routing — route CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW through write-capable subprocess (no -readonly) instead of through DuckDB.Service.run() which uses -readonly
Fix name collision — rename _ctx_file_relevance in either schema.duckdb.ts (TABLE) or duckdb-projection-worker.ts (VIEW) to avoid namespace conflict
Remove Effect.ignore chain in projection worker — log DuckDB errors properly instead of silencing them
Add health/telemetry metrics for DuckDB pipeline success/failure
Eliminate duplicate execDuckDB/execDuckDBStdin code between pipeline.ts and schema.duckdb.ts
Make :memory: mode either (a) work via in-process WASM DuckDB or (b) clearly document that context projections are unavailable
Add missing _ctx_agent_heatmap table definition or remove queryAgentHeatmap() dead code

packetFreshness.description: Fix externalChangesDetected to subtract agent-edited files from dirty set — use FileMemory.lastEditor or claims.owned
Add content-based freshness signal — include FileMemory digest checksums or git tree hash in Freshness type
Wire FileMemory into ContextPacket assembly — consult file digests to detect actual content changes in packet freshness calculation
